items.description: Dark ink numeral '64' written above the first line at top left.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval foliation mark added during cataloguing, rebinding, or institutional handling after the manuscript left Iceland (common in the post-1643 Danish royal and later Árni Magnússon Institute history of GKS 2365 4to).

items.description: Small isolated dark ink dot or short vertical stroke in the outer left margin, upper third of the page.
items.scholarly_note: Probable stray ink drop or minimal pen trial by the main scribe or a later reader; lacks connection to the text or decorative intent.

items.description: Large calligraphic initial letter at the opening of the page with elongated strokes extending toward the left margin.
items.scholarly_note: Original scribal decoration marking the start of a major section or stanza in Sigrdrífumál; standard hierarchical feature of the Codex Regius main hand.

items.description: Large 'L' initial beginning a new textual unit approximately two-thirds down the page.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal visual division for a stanza or sense unit within the poem; consistent with the manuscript's mise-en-page practices for Eddic verse.

items.description: Small isolated 'A' or letter-like mark visible below the final line of text toward the right side of the page foot.
items.scholarly_note: Function uncertain; possible quire or leaf signature, offset ink from the facing page, catch letter, or minimal later pen mark. Requires direct collation with adjacent folios.

items.description: Localized darker patches and minor ink smudges within the text lines in the middle and lower sections.
items.scholarly_note: Likely ink flow variation or minor pooling during writing; no clear evidence of deliberate correction, erasure, or expunction visible in available imaging.

items.description: Faint additional small dark speck or short mark on the far left edge, roughly mid-upper text block height.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly another stray mark, hairline offset, or natural vellum inclusion; not part of the main text or a developed marginal note.

scratches: Minor linear surface abrasions and hair-side wear visible in the lower text block and right margin under raw imaging.
Faint pressure lines or creases across the vellum consistent with handling, binding, or storage.
damage_notes: Age-related discoloration and soiling of the vellum, heaviest along the top edge, right margin, and especially the lower right corner.
Bottom right corner exhibits pronounced wear, darkening, soiling, and edge distortion with possible minor parchment loss or curling.
No large perforations, major repairs, or modern conservation interventions visible; the leaf remains intact with good ink legibility.
summary: Page 63 of Codex Regius contains a portion of Sigrdrífumál copied by the main scribe in Gothic book hand on well-prepared calf vellum. The surface is notably clean of figurative marginalia, doodles, animals, faces, or extensive pen trials. Visible features are limited to a later-added foliation numeral '64' in the upper margin, two large decorative initials marking internal textual divisions, one or two minimal stray ink marks in the left margin, a small ambiguous 'A' at the foot of the page, and localized ink variations within the text. The vellum shows typical 13th-century preparation with substantial peripheral staining and lower-right corner wear but no major holes, repairs, or scribal cancellations are apparent. The page is characteristic of the austere, text-centered presentation of the Poetic Edda in this codex.
